step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture separates online low-latency ad serving from offline batch processing and machine learning training. On the online side, a UserConsumerMobile client makes requests through an Application Load Balancer (ALB) to an ECS cluster running on EC2. The ECS application fetches user features from DynamoDB and invokes a SageMaker Model Endpoint to predict install probability. On the offline side, raw data is loaded from a bottom S3 bucket (Data Lake) into EMR, which aggregates and outputs processed datasets to a top S3 bucket. Data scientists access this processed dataset from SageMaker Notebooks to train and deploy models.

graph.name: Voodoo Mobile Ad Cross-Promotion Architecture
graph.notes: An AWS architecture that handles over 100 million model invocations daily to optimize cross-promotion of mobile games using Amazon ECS, DynamoDB, and SageMaker.

nodes.name: Mobile Gamer
nodes.notes: End-user playing a Voodoo game, triggering cross-promotion ad requests.

nodes.name: ALB
nodes.notes: Distributes incoming traffic to the ad serving backend.

nodes.name: ECS
nodes.notes: Runs the microservices that handle ad filtering and model requests.

nodes.name: EC2
nodes.notes: Provides the underlying compute instances for the ECS cluster.

nodes.name: DynamoDB
nodes.notes: Stores and retrieves real-time user features for predictions.

nodes.name: SageMaker Endpoint
nodes.notes: Hosts deployed ML models to return real-time ad install probability predictions.

nodes.name: Data Lake S3
nodes.notes: Stores raw analytical and transactional game data.

nodes.name: EMR
nodes.notes: Runs scheduled batch jobs to aggregate and join data sources.

nodes.name: Dataset S3
nodes.notes: Stores processed datasets ready for ML training.

nodes.name: SageMaker Notebook
nodes.notes: Used by data scientists to experiment, validate online performance, and run offline tests.

edges.notes: Routes traffic to ad serving container.
Involves SageMaker endpoint for prediction scoring.
EMR reads raw records from Data Lake.
Reads prepared datasets for model testing and training.
